title: Morphology-Driven Impedance Asymmetry of <mark>Lithium</mark> Metal During Stripping and Plating
authors: Chang-Xin Zhao, Yawei Chen, Xin Zhang and Chunsheng Wang
abstract: <mark>Electrochemical impedance</mark> spectroscopy (EIS) is a powerful tool for probing electrochemical systems. A rigorous understanding of the impedance behaviour of <mark>lithium</mark> metal electrodes is essential for analysing the kinetics of <mark>lithium</mark> metal batteries. Here, using a three-electrode configuration, we uncover a morphology-driven impedance asymmetry of <mark>lithium</mark> metal, in which pit-dominated stripping leads to high impedance, while dendrite-dominated processes result in low impedance. By analysing the impedance response and polarization behaviour of <mark>lithium</mark> metal electrodes in Li||Cu half-cells and Li|…
